summary: Apply simple practical rules while following current Trust training, signage and local instructions.
objectives: Know the practical limits of the volunteer role.
Follow current infection-prevention instructions and ward signage.
Know when not to attend or enter a patient area.

sections.title: Good intentions still need safe practice
body: Your Trust training and local induction provide the current detailed rules. Chaplaincy volunteers must follow ward signage and staff instructions, even when this means postponing a visit.
If guidance in this course ever appears to conflict with current Trust policy or a direct instruction from clinical staff, pause and check with chaplaincy.

do.title: Always
items: Wear your volunteer identification and follow local sign-in and sign-out arrangements.
Follow current hand-hygiene guidance on entering and leaving clinical areas and between contacts where instructed.
Follow the approved dress code, including bare-below-the-elbows requirements where applicable.
Ask staff when you are uncertain about access, precautions or a patient’s availability.

dont.title: Never
items: Enter an isolation area or pass a warning sign unless an appropriate member of staff has explicitly confirmed that you may do so and explained the precautions.
Move, lift, feed or give drinks to patients as part of the volunteer role.
Go behind closed bedside curtains or interrupt care, treatment or protected activity.
Attend while unwell or before the required symptom-free period in current Trust guidance has passed.

sections.label: Keep it current
sections.title: Signs and policies can change
body: This course deliberately avoids reproducing every infection-control sign or fixed return-to-volunteering interval. Follow your current mandatory training, local signage and advice from staff. Tell chaplaincy if any practical information in this course needs updating.

sections.label: On the ward
sections.title: A patient waves from an isolation room
sections.prompt: A patient you know waves and beckons you into a room displaying an infection-control warning. What should you do?

options.text: Enter briefly because the patient has invited you and looks lonely.
options.feedback: A patient’s invitation does not override infection-control precautions. Entering could place the patient, you and others at risk.

options.text: Do not enter. Check the current position with an appropriate member of staff and follow their instruction.
options.feedback: This protects everyone while allowing staff to explain whether and how contact might safely take place.

options.text: Ignore the patient and leave the ward without telling anyone.
options.feedback: You should not enter, but you can acknowledge the patient from outside if appropriate and ask staff or chaplaincy about safe alternatives.

sections.debrief: Compassion includes respecting precautions. A postponed or adapted visit is better than an unsafe one.
